finding: Gas CPU-guard counter WRAPS (AtomicU64 fetch_sub 0->u64::MAX); `prev==0` fires once then re-enables the guard for ~2^64 ops, so a catchable interrupt or the idle() drain phase runs CPU-unbounded. Confirmed by resource-limits AND concurrency angles.
Same gas-wrap defect: after exhaustion the counter wraps so a post-await while(true) inside idle() runs gas-unbounded, bounded only by the wall-clock.

gate() checks is_control_mutating BEFORE the Disabled case, so a mutating control tool inside a script under MCP-Disabled gets an approval prompt (and can run) instead of being flatly denied. Normal loop denies non-builtins under Disabled first. Confirmed by security AND perms angles.

gate() over-prompts read-only control calls under ManualApprove (normal loop auto-runs them via control_call_needs_approval=false). More-restrictive direction; and the gate unit test omits Disabled+control cases.

evaluate() runs inline (no spawn_blocking) and QuickJS runs synchronously in ctx.with, so a sync runaway pins a tokio worker; a catastrophic-backtracking regex (/(a+)+$/) has NO bytecode back-edges so NEITHER gas NOR the wall-clock cancel can fire — it pins a worker indefinitely and N such calls starve all workers.

run_js is NOT in the System-MCP hide-list queries nor the is_zero_config_builtin immutable set (unlike elicitation/skill/workflow/tool_result), so it appears as an editable admin row and an admin edit of its loopback url silently breaks attach.

runJsApprovalRequired drops the approval when streamingMessage is null (no else-branch, unlike mcpElicitationRequired); after reload/SSE-resume the suspended script's prompt is unreachable and it hangs until timeout.

resolveElicitation() catches all errors internally and never re-throws, and run_js registers no elicitationRequests entry, so the component's error rollback is dead code: a failed /respond POST shows a false 'Approved' while the script stays suspended. Confirmed by frontend AND api-contract angles.

Concurrent approvals (Promise.all of gated calls) render DUPLICATE static inner data-testids (run-js-approval-alert/-approve/-deny), colliding across simultaneous prompts and breaking strict-mode E2E selectors + unique-testid lint.

Reported error line is +3 off: wrap_script prepends 3 preamble lines and line_from_stack parses the wrapped line verbatim, so the model is pointed 3 lines below the real fault (a 1-line script reports line 4).

Config kill switch not enforced at the inline EXECUTION intercept — only at attachment. A disabled deployment keeps the enabled row, and mcp.rs execute_run_js_call has no is_enabled() guard, so a hallucinated/replayed run_js tool_use still executes.

No aggregate approval-wait cap: the watchdog pauses while pending>0, so a script issuing up-to max_tool_calls (100) sequential gated calls the user ignores can hold a 128MB runtime + task + SSE stream for ~100*300s (~8h). Confirmed by security AND resource-limits.

The wall-clock backstop cannot preempt a script parked in a host-future await; get_or_create_with_context (unlike execute_tool) has no timeout wrapper, so a hung session-establishment makes rt.idle() block past the advertised wall-clock.

128KiB output cap is enforced AFTER __ziee_set_result stores the full stringified value and from_str parses a second copy, so `return 'x'.repeat(60MB)` uses ~120MB uncounted host memory per call before truncation.

Tool-call budget caps total (100) but not CONCURRENCY: Promise.all of 100 gated calls fires up to 100 simultaneous code_sandbox/web_search dispatches per run_js invocation. Add a per-run concurrency semaphore.

Resolved status is local useState only; the injected block's content.status stays 'pending' and run_js registers no store entry, so a component remount (virtualized list / streaming->final key swap) re-shows an answered approval as interactive.
js_tool chat-extension order 29 COLLIDES with citations (also 29); every other attach-flag extension has a unique order, so registration order between the two is non-deterministic.

dispatch_one's non-ToolResult arm returns {value:null}+trace 'completed' (a false pseudo-success). Unreachable today (McpContentData has only ToolUse/ToolResult) but a silent-failure trap if the enum grows.
dispatch_one claims the call budget BEFORE the gate, so a denied/declined call still consumes a slot, inconsistently reducing capacity.
Denial message 'tool call {action}ed by user' yields 'declineed' for action=='decline'. Confirmed by correctness AND api-contract angles.
console byte-cap uses chars().take(remaining) (a CHAR count against a BYTE budget), so a multi-byte line can overshoot console_bytes on a single write.
__ziee_set_result is a bare global with first-writer-wins, so a script can pre-empt/hijack its own result (self-harm only, but violates the 'only the final return value reaches the model' invariant).
A poisoned result Mutex would misreport a completed run as a resource-limit kill (near-unreachable; robustness note). Recover via into_inner().

The elicitation registry entry leaks if request_approval is dropped by external task cancellation (not stream-close) while parked in select! (no RAII drop-guard). Matches the vetted ask_user pattern; a Drop guard closes it.
SSE field `server` is documented '(display name)' but the emitter passes server_name (internal name), not display_name. Cosmetic doc/label drift; frontend renders it fine.
jsonrpc_handler extracts ConversationIdHeader but never uses it (bound to _). Dead extraction vs the reference's purposeful use; retain-for-parity or drop.
host_bridge::install + the ZIEE_PRELUDE + the __ziee_dispatch JSON round-trip (the feature's CORE capability) has ZERO tests. Unit-testable via runtime::evaluate with a fake DispatchFn.

NO integration test exists (server/tests/js_tool/ absent): the headline claims (source='script' recording, approval suspend/resume via /respond, migration 134 grant, the mcp.rs intercept) are entirely unverified end-to-end.

request_approval's suspend/resume happy path (accept->Approved, decline/timeout->Denied, concurrent approvals) is untested; test_stream_closed_denies only hits the send-failure branch, never the select!.
test_memory_limit_enforced can't distinguish memory-cap from gas-cap firing (default gas + back-edge-heavy loop); set gas=MAX + a few-back-edge huge allocation + assert an OOM-specific message.
The interrupt-after-await gas path (post-await runaway killed inside idle(), the None fallback) is untested; test_cancel_flag_terminates uses gas=MAX so it tests cancel, not gas.

TEST-4 asserts only err.line.is_some() (tolerates a wrong line); line_from_stack is a pure parser with edge cases and is not directly unit-tested. Also unhandled-async-rejection and undefined-return->null are untested.

No server-wide cap on concurrent run_js runtimes; a burst of concurrent chat turns each calling run_js can drive aggregate (in-interpreter + uncounted host) memory arbitrarily high. Add a global admission semaphore.

Re-audit: the global admission semaphore held during approval-suspend blocked run_js server-wide for the suspend window (concurrency-cap fix converted 'one run held for hours' into 'all runs blocked'). FIXED: 15s bounded acquire -> fast busy result + spawn_blocking.

Re-audit: synchronous JS/regex runs on an async worker (gas drains 10000x slower in regex), starving async workers + the watchdog. FIXED: spawn_blocking isolates the interpreter; watchdog stays on the main runtime.
Re-audit: trace Vec grows once per DENIED dispatch without consuming the budget -> unbounded under a Disabled deny-loop. FIXED: MAX_TRACE_ENTRIES cap.
Re-audit: truncated_output false-positive when a script returns a value with a plain _truncated field. FIXED: collision-proof __ziee_truncated marker.
Re-audit: spawn_blocking non-cancellable -> global permit released while the eval lingers (admission under-count / pool-saturation on abort). FIXED: permit moved into the spawn_blocking closure.
Re-audit: gate() over-prompted read-only control tools under ManualApprove (normal loop auto-runs them). FIXED: gate() takes is_control; read-only control -> Allow.
Re-audit: addElicitationRequest overwrote an existing entry on double-delivery, resetting a resolved approval to pending. FIXED: guarded on !has(id).
